metadata.sources: DOE Building America, ENERGY STAR, RSMeans residential 2026, NAIMA, SPFA, RESNET, BPI, manufacturer pricing

materialTiers.notes: Cheapest option. Performance depends entirely on install quality — gaps and compression cut effective R 30-50%.
Standard for attic re-insulation. Settles 5-10% over 20 years.
Recycled paper + borate. Better air-sealing properties than fiberglass blown. Wets and clumps if roof leaks.
Mineral wool. Fire-resistant, sound-deadening, doesn't sag. ~2x cost of fiberglass batt.
Half-pound foam. Good air seal. Vapor permeable — must be installed correctly in cold climates.
Two-pound foam. Air + vapor barrier in one. Highest R per inch. Most expensive. Required for crawlspace encapsulation.
Pink/blue board. Used on basements, slabs, exterior walls.
Highest R per inch on rigid. R-value drops in deep cold — derate for climate zones 6+.

attic_top_off.label: Attic top-off (existing R-19/R-30 to R-49+)
attic_top_off.scope: Blow additional R-30 of fiberglass or cellulose over existing insulation in a 1,200-1,800 sqft attic.

wall_dense_pack.label: Wall dense-pack (drill and fill)
wall_dense_pack.scope: 1,500-2,000 sqft wall area, dense-pack cellulose blown into existing wall cavities through drilled holes.

crawlspace_encapsulation.label: Crawlspace encapsulation with closed cell foam
crawlspace_encapsulation.scope: Vapor barrier on floor + closed-cell spray foam on rim joist and walls. Optional dehumidifier add-on.

rim_joist_seal.label: Rim joist sealing with closed cell foam
rim_joist_seal.scope: 200-400 linear feet of rim joist, 2 inches closed-cell spray foam.

garage_door_insulation.label: Garage door insulation kit
garage_door_insulation.notes: Limited benefit unless garage is conditioned.

iraCredit.expirationNote: The 25C federal tax credit (30% of insulation materials, $1,200/year cap) EXPIRED Dec 31 2025. Insulation installs on or after Jan 1 2026 do NOT qualify for the federal credit. State utility rebates and IRA HEAR (income-qualified) programs are still active.

iraHearProgram.section: IRA Section 50122 — Home Electrification and Appliance Rebate (HEAR)
iraHearProgram.notes: HEAR is income-qualified and runs through state energy offices. Cannot be combined with the (now-expired) 25C on the same expenditure. Check your state energy office for HEAR rollout status.

utilityRebates.notes: Subsidized to as low as $0 for eligible homes after audit.

redFlagPatterns.explanation: Insulation work with no air-sealing mentioned. Insulation without air sealing loses 30-50% of its R-value. Reputable insulators always seal top plates, can lights, plumbing chases, and bath fans BEFORE adding insulation. Walk away if not addressed.
No R-value or depth specified. R-value is the only meaningful measure. Refuse any quote without specific R-value and depth — 'add insulation' without a target is meaningless.

Spray foam in habitable space without thermal barrier. IRC R316 requires a 15-minute thermal barrier (typically 1/2 inch drywall) over spray foam in habitable spaces. Skipping this is a fire-code violation.

Whole-home envelope work with no blower door test. Without before/after blower door numbers you cannot prove the work improved performance. Reputable home performance contractors always test in / test out.

Vermiculite (Zonolite brand) attic insulation is potentially asbestos-contaminated. Never disturb without licensed asbestos abatement testing first. Sweeping it aside or blowing over it can release fibers.

Attic insulation work with no ventilation review. Code requires 1 sqft net free vent area per 150-300 sqft of attic. Adding insulation without verifying vent area causes moisture buildup and ice dams.
No brand named for the insulation material. Brand and product line determine warranty and performance characteristics.

Spray foam quote with no installer certification. Bad spray foam installs (wrong ratios, off-ratio chemistry, contaminated tanks) cause permanent off-gassing and structural damage. Always verify SPFA Professional Certification or manufacturer training.
No workmanship warranty. Reputable insulation contractors offer 5-10 year workmanship warranties. Manufacturer material warranties are separate.
Sales rep claiming the 25C credit is still available. False — 25C expired Dec 31 2025. State utility rebates and IRA HEAR (income-qualified) may still apply but federal credit is gone.

scopeCheckItems.label: Vapor barrier strategy for climate zone

stateLicensing.notes: Insulation contractors are typically licensed under the state general contractor license. Spray foam often requires additional certification.

questionsToAskContractor: What target R-value and what material?
What air-sealing work happens BEFORE insulation goes in?
What is the existing attic ventilation and is it adequate?
Are you doing a blower door test before and after?
Is there any chance of vermiculite (Zonolite) in this attic? When was the home built?
If spray foam: are you SPFA certified? How will the thermal barrier be addressed?
What is the vapor barrier strategy for my climate zone?
Do I qualify for HEAR or any state utility rebates?
What brand and product line are you using?
